user_request: I need a basic REST API in Express.js that does CRUD for a todo list. Just something simple with an in-memory array, nothing fancy. I want endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and deleting todos.
user_goal: Build a minimal but functional REST API for a todo application using Express.js with in-memory storage

excellent_characteristics: All four CRUD endpoints implemented correctly
Proper HTTP status codes returned
Input validation on request body
Clean and idiomatic Express.js patterns
acceptable_characteristics: All four CRUD endpoints present and functional
minimum_requirements: At least three CRUD endpoints work

common_failures: Missing DELETE or PATCH endpoint
No error handling for missing todo IDs
Returning wrong HTTP status codes
edge_cases: Deleting a todo that does not exist
Updating with an empty request body
Creating a todo without a title field

user_request: Can you make me a React component that shows a list of products with a search bar at the top? When I type in the search bar it should filter the products in real time. Here's roughly what a product looks like: { name, price, category, inStock }.
user_goal: Create a React component with real-time search filtering over a product list

excellent_characteristics: Debounced search input for performance
Filters across multiple fields (name, category)
Accessible and semantic HTML structure
Clean component decomposition
acceptable_characteristics: Search filters product list in real time
Products render with all fields visible

common_failures: Search only filters by one field instead of multiple
Case-sensitive filtering confuses users
No empty-state message when no products match
edge_cases: Empty product array renders gracefully
Special characters in search input

user_request: I'm building a web scraper in Python and I need to fetch like 500 URLs concurrently but I don't want to hammer the server. Can you write an async solution using aiohttp that limits concurrency to 20 requests at a time and has retry logic for failed requests?
user_goal: Implement a Python async web scraper with bounded concurrency and automatic retry logic using aiohttp

excellent_characteristics: Uses asyncio.Semaphore for concurrency limiting
Exponential backoff retry strategy
Proper session management with context managers
Graceful error aggregation and reporting
acceptable_characteristics: Basic retry logic on HTTP errors
minimum_requirements: Handles at least basic HTTP errors

common_failures: Not properly closing aiohttp sessions
Retry logic without backoff causing thundering herd
Semaphore not actually limiting concurrent requests
Missing timeout configuration
edge_cases: All 500 URLs return 503 simultaneously
DNS resolution failures mid-batch
Connection pool exhaustion

user_request: We're running a multi-tenant SaaS app on PostgreSQL and our queries are getting slow with about 2 million rows per tenant. I need help designing the database schema with proper partitioning strategy and indexing. We have tenants, users, projects, and tasks tables. Can you walk me through the best approach?
user_goal: Design an optimized PostgreSQL schema with partitioning and indexing strategies for a multi-tenant SaaS application at scale

excellent_characteristics: Row-level security policies for tenant isolation
Hash or list partitioning by tenant_id
Composite indexes aligned with common query patterns
Explanation of trade-offs between partitioning strategies
acceptable_characteristics: Indexes recommended for major query paths
Schema DDL provided and syntactically correct
minimum_requirements: Valid PostgreSQL schema with tenant_id on all tables
At least one partitioning approach mentioned

common_failures: Suggesting sharding when partitioning suffices
Missing foreign key considerations across partitions
Ignoring tenant isolation and data leakage risks
edge_cases: Tenant with disproportionately large data volume
Cross-tenant reporting queries

user_request: Hey, I'm migrating our monolith e-commerce app to microservices. Right now I've got orders, inventory, payments, and notifications all tangled together. I need you to help me define the service boundaries, the communication patterns between them, and how to handle distributed transactions — especially the case where payment succeeds but inventory update fails.
user_goal: Define microservice boundaries and inter-service communication patterns for an e-commerce system with robust distributed transaction handling

excellent_characteristics: Saga pattern with compensating transactions defined
Clear bounded contexts for each service
Event-driven communication with message broker
Idempotency keys for payment retries
acceptable_characteristics: At least one distributed transaction pattern described

common_failures: Using two-phase commit instead of sagas for microservices
Not addressing eventual consistency implications
Overly fine-grained service decomposition
Ignoring data ownership between services
edge_cases: Payment gateway timeout with ambiguous status
Inventory reserved but order cancelled by user mid-process
Notification service down during order completion

user_request: I need a complete GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for a Node.js monorepo managed with Turborepo. It should run linting, unit tests, and e2e tests in parallel across affected packages only. Then deploy to staging on PR merge to develop, and production on release tags. Include caching for node_modules and Turborepo remote cache. Also need Slack notifications on failure.
user_goal: Create a production-grade GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with selective package testing, multi-environment deployments, intelligent caching, and failure notifications for a Turborepo monorepo

excellent_characteristics: Turborepo --filter flag for affected packages
Parallel job matrix for lint, unit, e2e
Proper GitHub Actions caching with hash keys
Environment-specific deployment with approval gates
acceptable_characteristics: Working YAML with lint, test, deploy stages
Conditional deployment based on branch or tag
minimum_requirements: At least lint and test jobs defined

common_failures: Cache keys not including lockfile hash
Missing permissions block for deployment
E2E tests not waiting for staging deployment
Slack notification step missing failure condition
edge_cases: No packages affected by a commit
Release tag pushed without PR merge

user_request: Write me Terraform modules for deploying a production-ready EKS cluster on AWS. I need a VPC with public/private subnets across 3 AZs, the EKS cluster with managed node groups that autoscale between 2-10 nodes, IAM roles with least privilege, and an ALB ingress controller. Please make the modules reusable with proper variable definitions.
user_goal: Create reusable Terraform modules for a production-grade AWS EKS deployment with networking, autoscaling, security, and ingress

excellent_characteristics: Modular Terraform structure with separate VPC, EKS, IAM modules
OIDC provider for pod-level IAM roles
Cluster autoscaler and ALB controller Helm releases
Outputs for kubeconfig and cluster endpoint
acceptable_characteristics: VPC with public/private subnets across 3 AZs
EKS cluster with managed node group and autoscaling

common_failures: Hardcoded values instead of variables
Overly permissive IAM policies (admin access)
Missing depends_on for resource ordering
edge_cases: AWS region with only 2 availability zones
Node group scaling to zero during off-hours

user_request: I have a Django REST API that handles user authentication and file uploads. Can you do a thorough security audit of this code? I want you to find vulnerabilities like injection flaws, broken auth, insecure file handling, CSRF issues, and anything else. For each vulnerability, explain the risk and give me the fixed code.
user_goal: Perform a comprehensive security audit on a Django REST API identifying all vulnerabilities with risk explanations and remediated code

excellent_characteristics: OWASP Top 10 mapped to findings
CVSS-style severity ratings per vulnerability
Before/after code for each fix
Defense-in-depth recommendations
acceptable_characteristics: Major vulnerabilities identified (SQLi, XSS, auth bypass)
minimum_requirements: At least one code fix provided

common_failures: Missing path traversal in file upload handling
Not checking for mass assignment vulnerabilities in serializers
Ignoring rate limiting on authentication endpoints
Overlooking insecure deserialization risks
edge_cases: Polyglot file uploads bypassing extension checks
JWT token reuse after password change

user_request: Our React Native app has terrible performance on Android — the FlatList with 10k+ items stutters badly, images flicker when scrolling, and navigation transitions are janky. The JS thread is consistently above 80% utilization. I need you to architect a complete performance optimization strategy including virtualization tuning, image caching pipeline, navigation optimization, and native module bridging where needed. Show me the actual code changes.
user_goal: Architect and implement a comprehensive performance optimization strategy for a React Native Android application suffering from rendering, scrolling, and navigation performance issues

excellent_characteristics: FlatList optimization with getItemLayout, windowSize, maxToRenderPerBatch
FastImage or custom native image caching module
React Navigation performance settings (detachInactiveScreens, freezeOnBlur)
Hermes engine and RAM bundle recommendations
acceptable_characteristics: At least two navigation optimizations applied

common_failures: Recommending web-only solutions that don't apply to React Native
Not addressing the Android-specific rendering pipeline
Suggesting memo/useCallback without profiling justification
edge_cases: Device with only 2GB RAM handling 10k list items
Offline image cache invalidation

user_request: Design a real-time collaborative document editing system like Google Docs that supports 500 concurrent editors per document with conflict-free resolution. I need the full architecture — CRDT vs OT decision with justification, WebSocket layer design, persistence strategy, cursor presence system, offline support with sync, and operational transform server. Target sub-100ms latency for character insertions. Please include code for the core CRDT/OT engine.
user_goal: Design and partially implement a real-time collaborative editing system with conflict resolution, presence awareness, offline support, and sub-100ms latency at scale

excellent_characteristics: Detailed CRDT vs OT comparison with justified selection
WebSocket fan-out architecture with room-based routing
Working CRDT implementation (e.g., RGA or Yjs-style)
Offline queue with vector clock synchronization
minimum_requirements: CRDT or OT approach selected and justified

common_failures: Choosing OT without addressing server bottleneck at 500 users
No strategy for cursor presence broadcasting at scale
Ignoring document size growth from tombstones in CRDTs
Missing garbage collection for CRDT metadata
edge_cases: Two users editing the exact same character simultaneously
User reconnecting after 24 hours offline with large diff
Document exceeding 1 million characters
